content.problemStatement:
Regenerate binary tree with given inorder and post order traversal. You need to return the pointer to the root node of the tree generated.

A binary tree is a tree data structure in which every node has at most 2 children nodes.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
First line of each testcase contains single integer NNN - number of nodes in Tree
Second line contains n space-separated integers In1,In2......Ini....InNIn_1, In_2......In_i....In_NIn1​,In2​......Ini​....InN​ - In Order Traversal
Third line contains n space separated integers Post1,Post2......Posti....PostNPost_1, Post_2......Post_i....Post_NPost1​,Post2​......Posti​....PostN​ - Post Order Traversal

All the values in In Order Traversal and Post Order Traversal are distinct.


**Note:**



For C++ language, you need to:

Complete the function in the submit solution tab:


`Node* regeneratedTree(vector In, vector Post){...}

`

Output Format
Using the functions you have completed, for each testcase pre order traversal of the tree will be compared for the correctness.



For each testcase N space separated integers Pre1,Pre2.......Prei...PreNPre_1, Pre_2 ....... Pre_i ... Pre_NPre1​,Pre2​.......Prei​...PreN​ - Pre Order Traversal will be outputted.
constraints: 1 <= N <= 10^5 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^5)
All node values are distinct and fit in 32‑bit signed integer.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains an integer N, the number of nodes in the tree.
- The second line contains N space‑separated distinct integers representing the inorder traversal.
- The third line contains N space‑separated distinct integers representing the postorder traversal.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing N space‑separated integers – the preorder traversal of the reconstructed binary tree.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case the reconstructed tree (shown in the statement) yields preorder 1 2 3 6 7 4 5. The second test case yields a tree whose preorder is 1 2 3 4 5.

solution.approach: The last element of the postorder segment is always the root of the current (sub)tree. Using a hash map we can obtain the index of this root in the inorder array in O(1). The inorder array is split into left and right parts, whose sizes give the corresponding ranges in the postorder array. Recursively repeat the process for left and right sub‑trees. While recursing we directly collect the preorder sequence (root → left → right). This runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(N) extra space for the map and recursion stack.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the number of nodes in that test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the hash map and recursion stack (worst‑case depth N for a skewed tree).

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def build(inorder, postorder, in_l, in_r, post_l, post_r, idx_map, preorder):
    if in_l > in_r or post_l > post_r:
        return
    root = postorder[post_r]
    preorder.append(root)
    pos = idx_map[root]
    left_sz = pos - in_l
    build(inorder, postorder, in_l, pos-1, post_l, post_l+left_sz-1, idx_map, preorder)
    build(inorder, postorder, pos+1, in_r, post_l+left_sz, post_r-1, idx_map, preorder)

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        inorder = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        postorder = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        idx_map = {val:i for i,val in enumerate(inorder)}
        preorder = []
        build(inorder, postorder, 0, N-1, 0, N-1, idx_map, preorder)
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, preorder)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
